message:
fix(pager): relax probe_visible_commit_seq receiver to shared ref and add missing semicolon in page_cache test

Two small correctness/style fixes in the pager crate:

1. pager.rs — probe_visible_commit_seq(&mut self → &self):
   The method only reads from self (file_size query, header byte reads,
   WAL begin_transaction + committed_txn_count, change-counter parsing).
   None of these operations require exclusive mutable access to PagerInner.
   Relaxing the receiver from &mut self to &self removes an unnecessary
   mutability requirement, which allows the method to be called without
   an exclusive borrow — enabling more flexible call-site ergonomics and
   making the API more honest about what it actually needs.

2. page_cache.rs — missing semicolon on assert! in test:
   The assert! macro invocation inside the None => branch of the
   cold-page admission eviction test was missing its trailing semicolon.
   While Rust allows the last expression in a block to omit the
   semicolon, this assert! is not in a tail position that benefits from
   that — the match arm is within a loop body. Adding the semicolon
   brings it in line with the surrounding assert! calls and prevents
   any future confusion about whether the expression's value is used.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
